title: Eva 111 and 222 on german free tv
display_title: Eva 111 and 222 on german free tv
wikitext:
[]: Eva 1.11 and 2.22 on german free tv             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Jul 12, 2012 7:23 am Just in case somebody from europe hasnt seen them yet or wants to tell somebody else to see it... Next week on sunday, July 22nd both movies will air back to back on Super RTL. Have fun ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Jul 12, 2012 7:42 am Thanks for the heads up. Will watch.

[]:             Quote Post by distopia » Fri Jul 13, 2012 9:29 am i would like to see these movies in German!

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Jul 13, 2012 10:44 pm Someone tape it and send it to me, my German is getting so rusty it's embarrassing.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Sun Jul 22, 2012 8:31 am Bump: 4 hours to go ;)

[]:             Quote Post by FriedlCore » Sun Jul 22, 2012 3:38 pm The german dub is pretty good!

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sun Jul 22, 2012 5:24 pm Yeah, it has one of the better dubs. Kind of dumb from Super RTL to cut out the credits and leave the part of Kaworu out.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Jul 22, 2012 5:32 pm Huh, turns out that I never saw 2.x in German before. Was actually an improvement over the series/1.x. Still hate Asuka's way too old voice as much as ever though... -_-

[]:             Quote Post by Restriktion » Mon Jul 23, 2012 2:09 pm It is nice to see so many members also apparently fluent in German here. I did watch it too, and actually, it was what made me sign up here. And i have to agree with you, that i think it was a good localized version. As for Asuka's voice. It may be that her voice in the original is somewhat softer, but it matches good with her precocious personality, don't you think?

[]:             Quote Post by FriedlCore » Mon Jul 23, 2012 2:14 pm Restriktion wrote: It is nice to see so many members also apparently fluent in German here. I did watch it too, and actually, it was what made me sign up here. And i have to agree with you, that i think it was a good localized version. As for Asuka's voice. It may be that her voice in the original is somewhat softer, but it matches good with her precocious personality, don't you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jul 24, 2012 2:03 pm Personally, I prefered the voices from the Tv series (Particularly Misatos VA) but I guess it's still a fairly good dub. And hey, they figured out how to pronounce "Asuka" this time. I dunno why those silent 'u's are always such I problem - Maybe it's because the *rest* of the letters/sounds are actually rather similar to how they're in German (at least compared to, hm, English or French) so that the actual differences get overlooked... Dunno. Of course, me and my sister watched it since I figured that I have to support it if I want more Anime on TV that's aired at a proper time/ isn't shredded to bits by RTL II. I was very happy that Gendo's and Shinji's VAs returned. Particularly Gendo's. I was sceptical about Mari's since that chick totally *ruined* Mariko of Elfen Lied, but I guess those roles require very different qualities so, nah, it was OK. It was also neat how they showed both movies directly after each other instead of leaving us waiting for a week and heck, it got advertizing and was really treated like a serious movie, it wasn't treated any different than any other SF film would've been for being animation. So yay, cool of them.
